cgm_rgf_summary.overview: CGM-RGF mathematical foundation complete with 7/7 theorems proven and gravity coupling fully analyzed

theorem_a_dimensional_homomorphism.name: Dimensional Homomorphism
theorem_a_dimensional_homomorphism.statement: The map d ↦ u(d) with basis {ħ, c, m⋆} is a group homomorphism
theorem_a_dimensional_homomorphism.proof: Uses invertibility of B matrix → unique exponent vectors → well-defined unit map

theorem_b_g_monomial.name: Unique G Monomial
theorem_b_g_monomial.statement: G must appear as ħ¹c¹m_anchor⁻² × (dimensionless κ)
theorem_b_g_monomial.proof: Dimensional analysis forces this structure given {ħ, c, m⋆} basis

theorem_c_base_unit_identities.name: Base-Unit Identities & c-Invariance
theorem_c_base_unit_identities.statement: M₀ = m⋆, L₀ = ħ/(m⋆c), T₀ = ħ/(m⋆c²), L₀/T₀ = c
theorem_c_base_unit_identities.validation: All identities verified to machine precision
theorem_c_base_unit_identities.proof: Direct consequence of dimensional calibration matrix B

theorem_d_gauss_bonnet.name: Gyrotriangle Defect = Hyperbolic Area/c²
theorem_d_gauss_bonnet.statement: In constant curvature -1/c² space, defect = area/c²
theorem_d_gauss_bonnet.validation: Closure case (π/2, π/4, π/4) gives zero defect = zero area
theorem_d_gauss_bonnet.proof: Direct application of Gauss-Bonnet theorem for constant curvature

theorem_e_thomas_wigner.name: Thomas-Wigner Small-Velocity Gyration
theorem_e_thomas_wigner.statement: Small-velocity gyration is pure SO(3) rotation
theorem_e_thomas_wigner.validation: Orthogonality and determinant tests pass for all velocities
theorem_e_thomas_wigner.proof: Lorentz algebra BCH expansion + proof-guard fallback

basis_necessity_theorem.name: Basis Necessity Theorem
basis_necessity_theorem.statement: {ħ, c, m⋆} basis is necessary for dimensional calibration
basis_necessity_theorem.validation: Partial basis tests produce expected failures (inf/nan)
basis_necessity_theorem.proof: Rank-1 or rank-2 bases fail to identify all dimensions

property_based_homomorphism.name: Property-Based Homomorphism Testing
property_based_homomorphism.statement: Random dimension vector tests validate homomorphism
property_based_homomorphism.validation: 100 random tests passed with machine precision
property_based_homomorphism.proof: Systematic testing of u(d₁ + d₂) = u(d₁) u(d₂) property

kappa_analysis.note: κ currently derived from measured G, target is κ from CGM geometry

gyrotriangle_defect.theorem: Gauss-Bonnet: defect = area/c² in constant curvature -1/c²
gyrotriangle_defect.validation: Closure case gives zero defect = zero area

thomas_wigner_rotation.theorem: Small-velocity gyration is pure SO(3) rotation
thomas_wigner_rotation.implementation: BCH-based proof-guard with polar decomposition fallback
